ops.title: Daemon validate-then-append race (accepted, repo-wide pattern)
ops.body:
Every daemon op that validates against reduced state before appending an event — `handleReply`'s unknown-block check (`internal/daemon/daemon.go`), `handleRound`, and the REST edge's `requireCurrentRound` (`internal/daemon/rest.go`) — runs `loadEvents → state.Reduce → check → appendEvent` with no lock spanning check and append. cc-interact's daemon spawns one goroutine per connection with no cross-request serialization (`cc-interact@v0.6.0/daemon/server.go:213-217`; `RepoLock` exists but serves cc-review snapshots and is unused here). A concurrent `remove-block`/`doc.replaced` can interleave, so a reply validated against a block can still land after the block is gone — an orphan interaction.

**Decision (2026-07-12, v0.6.1 review):** accepted, not fixed. Confirmed by an adversarial codex review pass but rejected in synthesis: the failure mode is a harmless orphan event (strictly better than the pre-0.6.1 unconditional append), and fixing only one handler would diverge from the repo-wide reduce-then-append idiom. A real fix means serializing per-subject check+append across all handlers (e.g. a per-subject mutex or single writer goroutine) — do it for all ops or none.
